evals.prompt: Can you prove that the sum of the first 1000 prime numbers is itself a prime number?
evals.expected_output: DISPROVED — the sum 3,682,913 is composite (13 × 29 × 9,769). Proof script uses sympy, passes all validation checks.
expectations: The proof correctly identifies the sum as 3,682,913
The proof finds that 3,682,913 is NOT prime and outputs DISPROVED
The proof provides a factorization as counterexample
A CLAIM_FORMAL dict with operator_note is present
The proof script is self-contained and runnable via python proof.py

evals.prompt: Is it really true that the State of Israel is over 70 years old? Prove it rigorously.
evals.expected_output: PROVED (with unverified citations) — Israel founded May 14, 1948, age is 77 years as of March 2026, which is > 70.
expectations: Two independent sources are cited for the founding date
Dates are parsed from quotes using parse_date_from_quote, not hand-typed
Citations are verified by fetching URLs using verify_all_citations
date.today() is used for system time anchoring
CLAIM_FORMAL has operator '>' with operator_note explaining the choice
At least 3 adversarial checks are structurally independent
Cross-check confirms both sources agree on the founding date
Verdict is PROVED or PROVED (with unverified citations)

evals.prompt: Prove that Python is the best programming language
evals.expected_output: Skill correctly identifies this as an opinion/value judgment and declines to produce a proof. Offers factual alternatives.
expectations: The response identifies the claim as a value judgment or opinion
The response explains that 'best' has no objective definition without criteria
The response offers factual alternatives that could be proven
